observations.recommendation:
We reiterate the following recommendations:
a. For the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:
i. Regularly monitors and evaluate the status of cash availability in relation to restricted cash balances such as continuing appropriation, obligations to be settled, and unobligated allotments to prevent further occurrence of cash deficits; and
ii. conduct reconciliation of records monthly or as needed to provide reliable financial information and ensure the accuracy of balances as well as continuing appropriations reported;
b. for the concerned officials to practice prudence in the disbursement of funds to minimize the existence of cash deficits
observations.reason: Lack of manpower, workforce

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, based on the foregoing audit observations, and to fairly present the Receivables Account, we recommended and the management agreed that:
a. in coordination with the concerned bank, intensify the reconciliation of affected loan accounts, to identify which party made the error and make the necessary adjusting entries, if applicable; b. henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledger for each loan type to monitor the movement of the book balances; c. conduct regular reconciliation of the loan accounts through proper coordination with the bank, and make the necessary actions therefrom; and d. make the necessary actions therefrom.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and workforce

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do the following: a) Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant promised to adhere the recommendation to conduct regular reconciliation of the loans payable between the book and bank balances and make the necessary action whether to adjust the books or inform the bank depending on the result of the reconciliation.
observations.reason: No reconciliation has been made as observed by auditors

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
a) the Local Chief Executive:
i. implement measures to balance the temporary assignment of BAC personnel to the GSO to effectively carry out its mandated functions, including property inventory, reconciliation, and disposal; and
ii. direct the existing members of the Inventory Committee to reorganize and reactivate the Inventory Committee by emphasizing the importance of their roles and encouraging renewed commitment to proper inventory management.
b) the General Service Officer develop and implement a strategic work plan that prioritizes key functions such as inventory-taking and property reconciliation.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct the concerned personnel to identify the real estates under her responsibility if there is a need to be registered under the Torrens Title System;
b) assign personnel to gather the supporting documents and establish the status of the real properties from the Registry of Deeds if necessary; and
c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to remove any entries reflecting depreciation or assigned useful life for land to prevent misstatements in asset valuation and to ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards and COA regulations.

We also reiterated to recommend that the General Service Officer:
d) expedite the processing of transfer of titles of pertinent LGU land parcels to its name, in order to ensure its legal ownership and completely protect its interest, in compliance with applicable rules and regulations; and
e) after registering the real properties in the LGU’s name, notify the Commission on Audit (COA) thereof in accordance with Article 47 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Accountant take up necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries upon the receipt of disposal reports from GSO to derecognized the unserviceable assets; and
b) the General Service Officer initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Manual of Disposal of Government Property and submit the report to Municipal Accountant for a proper derecognition of the PPEs in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
a) communicate and confirm long outstanding accounts to the source agency and identify if remaining funds from inter-agency fund transfers are to be reverted to the source agency, to the bureau of treasury or be retained by the municipal government; and
b) after confirmation, adjust the payable accounts if proven to be undocumented and without existing claimants especially those accounts which are outstanding for more than two (2) years as stated in Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer establish and enforce procedures that guarantee the timely submission of original cancelled checks to the Auditor as prescribed under Section 59 of NGAS, Volume I, Chapter 3.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant instruct the accounting personnel to ensure that payroll liquidation documents are only processed once all payees have properly signed to acknowledge receipt of their payments, in compliance with COA guidelines.
We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the payroll distributing officer to strictly enforce the requirement for payees to affix their signatures during the actual distribution of payroll to prevent incomplete documentation and to strengthen accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local School Board in coordination with Local Accountant:
a) revisit and strengthen existing internal policies and guidelines to ensure alignment with legal requirements;
b) ensure timely furnishing of SEF-related reports to all required agencies and local offices, complete with proof of receipt for monitoring and accountability; and
c) consistently comply with the reportorial and posting requirements on SEF allocation and utilization as mandated by applicable regulations.

We recommended and that the management agreed to strengthen the implementation of its GAD initiatives by enhancing gender mainstreaming efforts by:
a) First, members of the GFPS must be equipped with the necessary knowledge and skills for incorporating gender mainstreaming into the LGU's programs, projects, activities, and processes;
b) Second, a comprehensive and up-to-date GAD Database or SDD must be established and maintained as a foundation for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and
c) Lastly, the effectiveness of the project management processes employed by the LGU in relation to GAD initiatives must be refined by strengthening coordination and monitoring mechanisms.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to enforce the discontinuation of the practice of charging expenses listed in paragraph 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 against the 20% Development Fund (DF).

We reiterate our recommendation that the management:
a. strictly complies with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances and enforce all possible remedies to settle outstanding cash advances;
b. establishes a more stringent internal control over the granting of cash advances to all agency personnel and likewise on the liquidation thereof prior to their transfer or separation from the LGU; and
c. continues sending demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the LGU but whose whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed on the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive:
i. to optimize available personnel in the GSO, one-time cleansing program of PPEs is still ongoing and account balances, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020, to facilitate property reconciliation and validation of related account balances; and
ii. to prioritize availment of the one-time cleansing program for PPEs;
b. The Municipal Accountant:
i. in coordination with the concerned offices is still ongoing to adopt a regular reconciliation of records mechanism, with the view of ascertaining the actual status of the CIP accounts and the details necessary to identify the status of PPEs recorded under Trust Fund;
ii. prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components; and
iii. ensure that all concerned accounting staffs are well-informed of all applicable rules and regulations pertaining to the record-keeping on Local Road Networks.

To address the above observations, we recommended and the management agreed on the following:
a.) that the Municipal Accountant: i. administer the proper recording of disbursement based on the nature of related transaction and not merely based on the details of obligation of the request; ii. coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office and Requesting Office on the proper treatment of ambiguous transactions, to facilitate the necessary adjustments in the corresponding budget, if necessary, and to enforce submission of required supporting documents pertinent thereto; and iii. ensure, through proper monitoring and supervision, that the concerned accounting personnel verify and thoroughly review all supporting documents of travel claims pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ascertain that only valid ones are allowed;

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed on the following:
a.) to strictly prohibit designating Collecting Officers all employees eligible under Job Order Status, as soon as the budget for filling-in the regular vacant plantilla positions is made available; and
b.) meantime, as long as the workforce is not available, optimize distribution of workloads among available workforce under the MTO plantilla, to avoid, as practicable, designating duties of Municipal Collectors to JO employees.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed to the following:
a. For the Municipal Accountant to: i. facilitate the reclassification of improperly booked PPEs to their respective proper sub-account as applicable; ii. reconcile accounting records with the GSO records to foster proper monitoring, control and accountability over the PPEs; iii. ensure that all concerned accounting staffs are well-informed of all pertinent guidelines and regulations on the proper recording and accounting for PPEs; and
b. For the Municipal General Service Officer to: i. issue corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip to all personnel whom issued with semi-expendable items; and ii. conduct annual separate inventory-taking and prepare report thereon relative to the inventoriable supplies and materials such as semi-expandable items costing below ₱15,000] and small tangible items with estimated useful life of 1 year per Annex A of COA Circular No. 2005-002 dated April 14, 2005.

We recommended the following and the management agreed:
a) reconcile the discrepancies between the ending balances of CY 2021 unexpended QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE, as reported in the Report of Receipt and Utilization and the Notes to Financial Statement (NTFS), and effect necessary adjusting entry thereafter;b) in coordination with Municipal Budget to establish the ending balance of the current year’s DRRM appropriations at the end of the year to determine the amount to be transferred from General Fund to Trust Fund; c) transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRM account; and d) fast track the process of transferring the unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund.

Therefore, we reiterated and recommended the Municipal Accountant to: a. Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances and enforce all possible remedies to settle outstanding cash advances; b. Establish a more stringent internal control over the granting of cash advances to all agency personnel and likewise on the liquidation thereof prior to their transfer or separation from the LGU; c. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the LGU but whose whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables; d. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances; and e. After substantiation of the exhaustion of all applicable legal remedies to collect from outstanding receivables, determine the viability of applying the guidelines on requesting for write-off of receivables, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005, particularly on long outstanding and dormant receivable accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. regularly conduct reconciliation of reciprocal accounts, in order to detect errors and omissions, thus ensure accuracy therein; and
b. hereafter, administer the proper recording of transactions in the system to minimize errors and instruct the accounting personnel concerned to effect necessary adjustments to correct the affected account balances.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a. enroll the accounts of the LGU, in coordination with other officials, on the available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., WeAccess of LBP) to automatically check transaction history of the accounts which could be used to prepare the BRS; b. formalize promptly the closure of the dormant accounts, if warranted; and c. enroll the accounts of the LGU, in coordination with other officials, on the available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., WeAccess of LBP) to facilitate checking of transaction history of the accounts, thus, easily prepare the BRS.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed to the following:
a. For the General Service Officer to: i. after registering the real properties in the LGU’s name, notify the Commission on Audit (COA) thereof in accordance with Article 47 of the Local Government Code; and
b. For the Municipal Accountant to book the unrecorded land owned by the LGU in the books of accounts, consistent with the standards set under IPSAS 17, to arrive at reasonable balances of land and government equity accounts.
observations.reason: Documents and other legal matters are being processed.

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, based on the foregoing audit observations, we recommended the management agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal General Service Officer to: a. prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network; b. maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and c. maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
2. For the Municipal Accountant to: a. ensure that all concerned accounting staffs are well-informed of all applicable rules and regulations pertaining to the record-keeping on Local Road Networks; b. prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components namely: road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures and other miscellaneous structure; c. prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each road component; and d. disclose accordingly the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
3. For the Municipal Engineer to: a. assist the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant in conducting the physical inventory of the Local Road Network in order to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network; and b. provide Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken, for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.
4. For the Municipal Assessor to help in determining the costing of the land where the local road networks are erected.

We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
a. To the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO, they agreed to verify the veracity of all data with discrepancies, as indicated in Tables 20-22, and after proper reconciliation procedures, effect necessary adjustments therefor.
b. To the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO, and they agreed to henceforth establish and conduct regular reconciliation of all LDRRMF components, such as the current year appropriation, STF and Continuing Appropriation.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed to do the following: a) Enroll the accounts of the LGU, in coordination with other officials, on the available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., WeAccess of LBP) to automatically check transaction history of the accounts which could be used to prepare the BRS; b) formalize promptly the closure of the dormant accounts, if warranted; and c) enroll the accounts of the LGU, in coordination with other officials, on the available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., WeAccess of LBP) to facilitate checking of transaction history of the accounts, thus, easily prepare the BRS.

Therefore, we recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to do the following: 1. Conduct disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set in the HSPMS. If budget permits, provide additional staff to give attention on the inventory and disposal of unserviceable properties thereon, for proper dropping in the books of accounts. 2. After the disposal, submit the necessary documents to the Office of the Accountant as basis of the latter in dropping the PPEs in the books of accounts. Also, make extra effort in reconciling PPEs already disposed but were not yet dropped in the books of accounts.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to do the following: a. For the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to: i. regularly monitor and evaluate the status of cash availability in relation to restricted cash balances such as continuing appropriation, obligations to be settled and unobligated allotments to prevent further occurrence of cash deficits; b. For the Municipal Treasurer to analyze collection shortfalls/deficiencies to identify and implement revenue-enhancing strategies to further improve collection efficiency;

Provide the GSO with proper storage facility to safe keep the office bodega for waste materials or unserviceable properties of the Municipality of Tantangan. Refer to PSMS Handbook on the proper storage planning and layout. Require the assigned personnel in the GSO to attend Property and Supply System Management Seminar or refer to its manual so that proper storage and warehousing is attained.

Require the assigned personnel in the GSO to attend Property and Supply System Management Seminar or refer to its manual so that proper storage and warehousing is attained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
with the involvement of the Local Chief Executive as the head of the agency:

a. ensure that only bonafide obligations of the agency are included and
accurately recorded in the books of accounts;

b. coordinate with the creditor bank regarding loan records to properly
reflect the correct balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic Account of the
Municipality; and

c. henceforth administer the proper recording of transactions in the system
and instruct the assigned personnel to prepare the adjusting entries to
reflect the correct/accurate balances of the aforementioned accounts.

We reiterated and Management agreed on the following recommendations:

a) for the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:

i. regularly monitor and evaluate the status of cash availability in
relation to restricted cash balances such as continuing appropriation,
obligations to be settled, and unobligated allotments to prevent
further occurrence of cash deficits;

ii. conduct reconciliation of records monthly or as needed to provide
reliable financial information and ensure the accuracy of balances as
well as continuing appropriations reported;

b) for the concerned officials to practice retrains and prudence in the
disbursement of funds to minimize the existence of cash deficits.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer enhance forecasting of
estimated revenue to ensure that collection thereof could be realized;

b) the Local Finance Committee employ trend analysis of actual income collected
and consider the results during budget planning to ensure realistic and feasible
budget;

c) the Municipal Treasurer analyze collection shortfalls/deficiencies to identify
and implement revenue-enhancing strategies to further improve collection
efficiency; and

d) the Local Chief Executive adopt a more conservative budget to minimize the
accumulation of cash deficit and ensure proper funding of different projects,
programs, and activities embodied in the annual budget.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:

a. strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances and enforce all possible remedies to settle outstanding cash
advances;

b. establish a more stringent internal control over the granting of cash
advances to all agency personnel and likewise on the liquidation thereof
prior to their transfer or separation from the LGU;

c. continue sending demand letters to officials and employees who are no
longer connected with the LGU but whose whereabouts are still known to
liquidate their cash advances/ receivables;

d. remind the employees regarding the strict compliance of liquidation of
cash advance for travel on time according to the circular as mentioned
above;

e. conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the
cash advances; and

f. after substantiation of the exhaustion of all applicable legal remedies to
collect from outstanding receivables, determine the viability of applying
the guidelines on requesting for write-off of receivables, as indicated in
COA Circular No. 2016-0053, particularly on long outstanding and
dormant receivable accounts.

We recommended that:

a. the Municipal Accountant coordinate with Municipal Budget to establish
the ending balance of the current year’s DRRM appropriations at the end
of the year to determine the amount to be transferred from General Fund
to Trust Fund;

b. the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and
MF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability- DRRM
account. The following journal entries are suggested:

Account Name Debit Credit
In the General Fund book, the entry is:
Transfers from GF of Unspent DRRMF to the TF Xxx
Cash in bank -– LCCA Xxx
While in the Trust Fund book, the entry would be:
Cash in bank -– LCCA Xxx
Trust Liability-DRRMF Xxx

c. the Municipal Treasurer fast track the process of transferring the
unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund.

We reiterate to recommend that Management:

a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to verify the veracity
of all data with discrepancies, as indicated in Tables 3-4, and after proper
reconciliation procedures, effect necessary adjustments therefor;

b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies
between the ending balances of CY 2023 unexpended QRF and
Mitigation Fund-MOOE, as reported in the Detailed and Summarized
Reports of Receipt and Utilization and the Notes to Financial Statements
(NTFS), and effect necessary adjusting entry thereafter;

c. require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the LDRRMO, to
henceforth, establish and conduct regular reconciliation of all
LDRRMF components, such as the current year appropriation, STF and
Continuing Appropriation.

d. instruct Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to
prepare and submit Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
reconcile the balances of the CIP accounts with the pertinent records of Engineering
and other offices, to determine the status of remaining balances, and immediately
transfer amounts pertinent to completed PPAs to the proper PPE accounts, and
accordingly record depreciation therefor.
We, also, recommended and the Municipal Engineering Office agreed to
provide periodically the Accounting Office of the implementation status of
infrastructure projects undertaken by the LGU, to assist in the periodic review and
monitoring of the pertinent CIP balances thereof. The status report must be
supported with Certificate of Completion on completed PPAs and Statement of Work
Accomplished on on-going PPAs.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed to the
following:
a. For the Municipal Accountant to:
i. facilitate the reclassification of improperly booked PPEs to their respective
proper sub-PPE accounts, Inventory Account (for semi-expendable items),
as applicable;
ii. record the semi-expendable items as expense upon issuance thereof, in
accordance with the pertinent guidelines;
iii. reconcile pertinent accounting PPE records with the relevant GSO records
to foster proper monitoring, control and accountability over the PPEs;
iv. ensure that all concerned accounting staffs are well-informed of all
pertinent guidelines and regulations on the proper recording and
accounting for PPEs, and likewise ensure the accuracy and completeness
of PPE-related details entered into the accounting system, to facilitate
proper monitoring and recording; and
v. follow-up request to the service provider to address promptly all system
issues, in order to reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances with the
Financial Statement as well as to facilitate proper reclassification of PPE
items.
b. For the Municipal General Service Officer to:
i. issue corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip to all personnel whom
issued with semi-expendable items; and
ii. conduct annual separate inventory-taking and prepare report thereon
relative to the inventoriable supplies and materials such as semi-
expandable items costing below ₱15,0001 and small tangible items with
estimated useful life of 1 year per Annex A of COA Circular No. 2005-
002 dated April 14, 2005.

With the foregoing, we recommended the following and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to:
a. henceforth, extensively review the accounting of all pertinent components of
LDRRMF, more particularly those assigned to staff, and ensure that the
corresponding balances thereof are reconciled at reporting dates;
b. discontinue the practice of transferring unexpended current year
appropriation for Capital Outlay to STF-DRRM, in accordance with the
pertinent provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
c. reconcile the discrepancies between the ending balances of CY 2021
unexpended QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE, as reported in the Report of
Receipt and Utilization and the Notes to Financial Statements (NTFS), and
effect necessary adjusting entry thereafter.

Therefore, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the
management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
a. in coordination with the concerned bank, intensify the reconciliation of the
affected loan accounts, to identify which party made the error and make
the necessary adjusting entries, if applicable;
b. henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledger for each loan type to monitor the
movement of the book balances for easy reconciliation of loan accounts
with the bank balances;
c. update the email account registered in the bank to where the bank memos
and other relevant correspondence will be sent;
d. henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of the loan accounts through
proper coordination with the bank, and make the necessary actions
therefrom; and
e. present the current portion of long-term loans as Loans Payable- Current
in the financial statements as of year-end, in compliance with the pertinent
guidelines.

Therefore, based on the foregoing audit observations, and to fairly present the
Receivables Account, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash
advances and enforce all possible remedies to settle outstanding cash
advances;
b. Establish a more stringent internal control over the granting of cash
advances to all agency personnel and likewise on the liquidation thereof
prior to their transfer or separation from the LGU;
c. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer
connected with the LGU but whose whereabouts are still known to
liquidate their cash advances/ receivables;
d. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the
cash advances; and
e. After substantiation of the exhaustion of all applicable legal remedies to
collect from outstanding receivables, determine the viability of applying
the guidelines on requesting for write-off of receivables, as indicated in
COA Circular No. 2016-0054, particularly on long outstanding and
dormant receivable accounts.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed to undertake the
following measures to be carried out by the Municipal Accountant to improve the
reconciliation process between the book and bank balances to present fairly the Cash
in Bank-LCCA account in the Financial Statements:
a. substantiate, reconcile, and record necessary adjusting entries for valid
book reconciling items as reflected in the BRS, such as unrecognized bank
debits and credits, and long-existing unreconciled items, among others;
b. henceforth, regularly request for copies of debit and credit memos from
the bank for proper and prompt recording thereof in the books of
accounts; and
c. facilitate the prompt evaluation and cancellation of the stale checks, as
reported in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) in coordination with
the Municipal Treasurer, and reissue new checks to the payees or reinstate
instead the appropriate liabilities accounts, as applicable.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. regularly conduct reconciliation of reciprocal accounts, in order to detect
errors and omissions, thus ensure accuracy therein; and
b. hereafter, administer the proper recording of transactions in the system to
minimize errors and instruct the accounting personnel concerned to effect
necessary adjustments to correct the affected account balances.

With the foregoing, we reiterated our previous recommendation and the
management agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare bank reconciliation reports on a monthly basis for all depository
accounts of the LGU to facilitate reconciliation and fair presentation
thereof in the Financial Statements;
b. retrieve documents from the Municipal Treasurer on the closed and
dormant accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books; and
c. evaluate the need to augment the manpower of the accounting department,
and act accordingly by making representation to the concerned offices, or
otherwise strategize distribution of tasks to available manpower to
facilitate prompt and proper preparation of BRS, among others.
2. For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. submit to the Municipal Accountant and Audit team all documents relating
to the closure of the accounts in LBP-main office, such as the Sanggunian
Resolutions for the closure, among others;
b. formalize promptly the closure of the dormant accounts, if warranted; and
c. enroll the accounts of the LGU, in coordination with other officials, on the
available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., WeAccess of LBP) to
facilitate checking of transaction history of the accounts, thus, easily
prepare the BRS.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed to the
following:
a. For the Municipal General Service Officer to:
i. expedite the processing of transfer of titles of pertinent LGU land parcels
to its name, in order to ensure its legal ownership and completely protect its
interest, in compliance with applicable rules and regulations; and
ii. after registering the real properties in the LGU’s name, notify the
Commission on Audit (COA) thereof in accordance with Article 47 of the
Local Government Code.

in the books of accounts, consistent with the standards set under IPSAS 17, to

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed on the
following:
1. For the Municipal General Service Officer to:

Network;

its components; and

2. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure that all concerned accounting staffs are well-informed of all
applicable rules and regulations pertaining to the record-keeping on Local
Road Networks;
b. prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and
its components namely: road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope
protection structures and other miscellaneous structure;
c. prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each
road component; and
d. disclose accordingly the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial
Statements.
3. For the Municipal Engineer to:

Road Network in order to come up with the Report on the Physical Count
of Local Road Network; and

description and cost segregation of road components of every road project
undertaken, for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the
Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property
Card, respectively.
4. For the Municipal Assessor to help in determining the costing of the land
where the local road networks are erected.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed that the BAC
Chairman and the Municipal Engineer henceforth ensure/require the posting of
Warranty Securities before final payment of projects in all infrastructure projects as
protection against possible structural defects/failures that may occur.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

and

final billings, as enumerated in Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular
2012-001, are submitted by the contractor and attached to the vouchers
before payment thereon.

following:

respectively; and

preparation of LDRRMFIP aside from the annual work and financial
plan, and necessary amendments or supplemental thereon for all
prospective changes in DRRM-PPAs, in accordance with the prescribed
format set forth in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002

following:

verify the veracity of all data with discrepancies, as indicated in Tables

b. To the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO, and

of all LDRRMF components, such as the current year appropriation,
STF and Continuing Appropriation.

allocation to disaster-response, and ensure that the LDRRMF are
optimally utilized in accordance with RA 10121.

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, we recommended the following and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do the following to present fairly the receivable accounts of the Municipality in the Financial Statements:
a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose which deprives the government of funds had these receivables been collected and affecting the fairness of the Financial Statements.
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still connected with the Municipality and stop the granting of additional cash advances if previous cash advances have not been liquidated
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular 1997-0021 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the Municipality but whose whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables and consider taking legal actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of the named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write off of the receivables, in accordance with existing regulations, for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the clearances. Exclude for request for write-off individuals whose receivable arise from disallowances and charges, cash shortages and subject of a pending case in court or before investigative authorities.

e. Investigate, reconcile and make the necessary adjustment for the accounts subject for reconciliation and erroneous recording

present the current portion of long-term loans as of year-end as Loans
Payable- Current in the financial statements in compliance with Section
367 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs Volume I, for
purposes of fair and proper presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following:
• Constantly, follow-up the Municipal Treasurer on the issuance of
check for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF and
70% Mitigation Fund to the Trust Fund for DRRM
• Transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF-MOOE to the
Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability- DRRM account as of
year end

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following:
• Make necessary effort to provide information of the PPEs not
provided with depreciation by coordinating with other
Offices/Departments and provide the depreciation if complete data
has been gathered;
• Check the accuracy of information entered in the accounting system
and make sure all data of the PPEs are provided;
• Recompute the computation of depreciation once entered into the
system to verify and establish the correct amount;
• Transfer completed and long outstanding PPEs of trust fund to the
General Fund and provide depreciation on these PPE.
• Contact the service provider of the accounting system so that
correct and complete details of the PPE in system will be provided
and ensure correct calculation of depreciation on these properties
• Make adjustments on the presentation of properties which were
reflected with over provision of depreciation.

Therefore, we recommended and the General Service Officer agreed to do
the following:
 Conduct disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with
the guidelines set in the HSPMS. If budget permits, provide
additional staff to give attention on the inventory and disposal of
unserviceable properties
 After the disposal, submit the necessary documents to the Office of
the Accountant as basis of the latter in dropping the PPEs in the
books of accounts
 Continue reconciling with the record of the Municipal Accountant
with regards to the already disposed PPEs for proper dropping in the
books.

9.9 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant, after the disposal,
agreed to draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the properties and
apply appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the
books. Also, make extra effort in reconciling PPEs already disposed but
were not yet dropped in the books of accounts.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive, through the
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, BAC Chairman and other
personnel concerned, agreed to do the following:
• Discontinue/stop the practice of procuring goods through cash
advances and reimbursements
• Instead of Cash Advances, consider GPPB 06-200 dated March 25,
20204 on advanced payments to supplier for mobilization adhering
to needed supporting documents as embodied in the Circular
• Conduct the procurement activities in accordance with the
provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and guidelines
provided by GPPB resolutions, specifically during state of calamity
• Make the supplier accomplish the Omnibus Sworn Statement
(OSS) and attach the same to the Disbursement Voucher for
procurements undertaken under Bayanihan to Heal as One Act

Therefore, we recommended and the Management, through the
Sangguniang Bayan, agreed to comply with the existing provisions as
regards the realignment expenditures to ensure that legislation enacted is
sufficient and appropriate in accordance with the provision of law.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
direct the Local School Board of the Municipality to;
a. Formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization
policies and to coordinate with the concerned National Government
Agencies and the Provincial School Board, to carry out the planning
and budgeting activities for the SEF;
b. Prepare the SEF budget using the SEF Budget Preparation Form
No. 1 as stated in the JC;
c. Incorporate to the SEF budget the ECCD programs and provide
specific activities and projects following the purpose of the funding
as stated in Section 4 of the JC; and
d. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the schools
concerned and other stakeholders. Thereafter, consolidate all
budget requests from all schools in the LGU to have a sound
scheduled activities that will be funded out of the SEF in
consideration with the duly funded programs of the DepEd
National Office

Had all collections been recorded as part of the control mechanism to ensure
proper presentation of amounts to Financial Statements, all accounts could have
been presented without misstatements.

Besides, it is clear that accurate, comprehensive and reliable financial
information is fundamental to accountability and decision-making in the public
sector. Erroneous presentation of the balances would mislead users of the
information in making sound decisions.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following
to ensure proper control and presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements:

For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the adjusting entries to correct the misstatement of the Cash-
Local Treasury account
b. Instruct the accounting personnel concerned to record all collections
relating to all other taxes, fees, charges and other revenues in a cash
basis of accounting.
For the Municipal Mayor:
a. Provision for additional personnel at the Municipal Accounting Office,
preferably a Certified Public Accountant, to assist the Municipal
Accountant in the analysis of accounts to trace and adjust
misstatements of accounts.

Therefore, we recommended the following:
For the Municipal Accountant to:
• Prepare bank reconciliation report on a monthly basis for every
depository account of the Municipality to accurately present the
balances of the account in the Financial Statements.
• Retrieve documents from the Municipal Treasurer on the closed
accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books
• Make the necessary adjustment in the books upon receipt of the
updated documents relative to the closure of the dormant accounts.
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Submit to the Municipal Accountant and Audit team all documents
relating to the closure of the account in LBP-main office including,
but not limited to Sanggunian Resolution for closure and transfer
to LBP-Gaisano Branch, applicable Auto-Debit Arrangement
(ADA) documents, opening of accounts and others.
• Update the signatories of the non-moving accounts if needed and
recommend for the closure of the dormant accounts.
• Ensure to gather all needed documents for closing the dormant
accounts
Management’s comment:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant commented that the variance
is material however they will look into the discrepancy by updating the complete
preparation of the bank reconciliation statement of each bank accounts maintained by the
Municipality. This, he added, would relatively shed light on the difference noted in audit.
It was further mentioned that the lack of manpower, specifically of financial expertise,
really contributed on the partial preparation of the bank reconciliation. The management,
nevertheless, assured that proper controls and attention will be given to implement the
recommendations.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following
measures to be carried by the Municipal Accountant to present fairly the
Cash in Bank accounts in the Financial Statements:
a. Locate and trace down the documents to support the adjusting entries
on the long outstanding reconciling items
b. Request copies from the bank of the debit memos and credit memos
issued for proper recording in the books of accounts;
c. Record adjustments for valid reconciling items per books as reflected
in the BRS prepared;
d. Locate the stale checks if it is still with the Municipal Treasurer and
make the necessary adjustments. If the check is not available, confirm
the payable account with the payee and make the proper procedures
for replacing the check. If no response was received, make the
necessary adjustments with the books.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to do
the following:
a. Instruct the Local Finance Committee to employ trending analysis of
data to facilitate realization of the reasonable targeted budget and
ensure proper management of financial affairs of the Municipality.
b. For the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
regularly monitor and evaluate the status of cash availability in relation
to restricted cash balances such as continuing appropriation,
obligations to be settled and unobligated allotments to prevent further
occurrence of cash deficits
c. For the Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate with Municipal
Treasurer on the availability of cash before declaration of allotments
and accordingly, present fairly budget report.
d. For the Municipal Treasurer to analyze collection
shortfalls/deficiencies to identify and implement revenue enhancing
strategies to further improve collection efficiencye. For the accountable officers to practice restraints and prudence in the
disbursement of funds

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to do the
following:
• Instruct collectors to stop the accommodation payments as well as use
of collection for personal purposes.
• Require collectors on field, particularly those collections related to
speed limit violations, be remitted the next day
• Change the scheme of the remittance of the collection of collectors to
the Liquidating Officer in accordance to Section 29 of NGAS Volume
1, that is, to remit to the Liquidating Officer all collections made during
the day at the close of each business day for collectors situated in the
Municipal Treasury’s Office or near the Municipality’s Office and as
soon as the collections reach P5,000.00 for those Collectors whose
official station will take more than one day to travel.
• Instruct the Liquidating Officer to deposit collections within the day or
the next banking day.
• Stop the practice of simultaneously releasing accountable forms

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following:
• Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to
reclassify the real property tax discounts from Other Prepayments
account to Discount on Advance Payment account. Below is the
suggested entry:
GF Book Amount
DR Discount on Advance Payment P1,657,560.48
CR Other Prepayments P1,657,560.48
• Require the personnel in-charge to determine the amounts of discount
on advance payment under the SEF book and prepare the necessary
accounting adjusting entries to book up the amount.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following to present fairly the receivable accounts of the Municipality
in the Financial Statements:
a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to
ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable
and that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period
depending upon their nature and purpose
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding
cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still
connected with the Municipality
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 1997-
0024 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the
officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to
comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against
them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer
connected with the Municipality but whose whereabouts are still
known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables and consider taking
legal actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of the
named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already
deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known
(e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write
off of the receivables, in accordance with existing regulations, for fair
presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into
consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the
clearances. Exclude for request for write-off individuals whose
receivable arise from disallowances and charges, cash shortages and
subject of a pending case in court or before investigative authorities.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant will do the
following to further improve reliability and accurateness of financial
reporting:
a. Confirm the amount of the payables from the list of the identified
creditors to determine which of the payables are still valid and bonafide
obligation and make necessary adjustments in the books
b. Adjust the payable accounts if proven to be undocumented and without
existing claimants especially to those accounts which are outstanding
for more than 2 years as stated in Section 98 of PD 1445
c. Review the validity of accounts payable in a regular basis to promptly
determine which of the accounts are already dormant and to take
necessary remedial measures
d. Investigate, reconcile and make the necessary adjustment for the
accounts subject for reconciliation and erroneous recording
Management’s comment:
The Municipal Accountant, during the exit conference, commented that P3.2M of
the long outstanding payables referred to the unused fund transfer from Department of
Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) intended for skills trainings of the constituents
but were not implemented due to unavailability of skills trainings in schools located in the
Municipality. Nevertheless, they agreed to refer the matter with DSWD and make amends
on how to utilize the amount transferred for the benefit of the constituents of the
Municipality. They further commented that they were amenable on the audit findings and
certain measures will be adapted to comply with the recommendations.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
exert effort to retrieve the supporting documents relative the JEV and
analyze the entry. After analysis, proper adjustments will be undertaken
to present the accounts fairly in the Financial Statements.

Moreover, management agreed on the requirement that supporting
documents relative to the account be submitted to the audit team for
further analysis.

Therefore, we recommended the following:
For the Municipal Accountant to:
• Coordinate with the MGSO to reconcile items for reconciliation
and the difference noted between the books and the physical count
• Exert effort to reconstruct the list of the PPE accounts which are
for reconciliation coordinating closely with other departments
especially MGSO for proper substantiation of accounts;
• Ensure that items indicated in the subsidiary ledger is coined as to
its proper details to ensure proper matching of records with the
MGSO
• Transfer completed and long outstanding PPEs of Trust Fund to
the General Fund and subject the same to depreciation
• Provide complete details of the PPE in the accounting system
utilized to ensure calculation of depreciation on all properties of
the Municipality
• Provide depreciation on all PPEs subjected for depreciation as
considered in accounting parlance
• Review, verify and make the necessary adjustments on properties
under 20% development fund with accumulated depreciation
more than its acquisition cost
For the Municipal General Services Officer to:
• Put stickers on physically counted properties with complete details
• Adjust records based on the results of the conduct of the physical
count
• Reconcile records with the Accounting Office
• Require the accomplishment of Property Return Slips, Waste
Material Reports, Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment6 and
other documents for the return of unserviceable properties from
the accountable officer to MGSO or from one accountable officer
to another
• Renew Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment least once in
every three years or every time there is a change in accountable
officer or custodian of the property.
For the Municipal Mayor to:
• Augment manpower needs of the MGSO, if budget permits
• Send MGSO personnel to proper training / seminar on
Government Management and Disposal of properties
• Provision for bodega for storage of unserviceable properties
Management’s comment:
The management was amenable on the audit observation and promised to undertake
the audit recommendations. They added, during the exit conference, that a store room was
already provided for some of the unserviceable properties of the Municipality to secure
them from further wear and tear. However, they acknowledged that it was not enough to
store all unserviceable properties thus they are in the onset of the process of disposing
identified unserviceable properties.

Therefore, to have a more reliable financial reporting and effective
property management of the agency over the assets. We recommended and
management agreed to do the following:
a. For the Property Custodian to:
• Strictly observe the utilization of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS)
upon the issuance of small tangible items for proper monitoring and
accountability
• Utilize ICS for semi-expendable9 items costing P10,000.00 and
below for monitoring and accountability purposes
• Conduct annual separate inventory-taking and report for all
furniture, fixtures, equipment, books and work animals costing
P10,000.00 and below (semi-expendable items) and small tangible
items
b. For the Municipal Accountant to:
• Review the PPE list to identify items needed to be excluded from the
PPE accounts relative to the small tangible items and semi-
expendable items
• Make proper adjustments in the books on identified capitalized
small tangible items and semi-expendable items
• Coordinate with the MGSO for the updating of inventory reports
and reconciliation.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following:
• Coordinate with Municipal Budget to establish the ending balance of
the current year’s DRRM appropriations at the end of the year to
determine the amount to be transferred from General Fund to Trust
Fund.
• Transfer the unexpended/unobligated QRF and MF-MOOE to the
Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability- DRRM account. This can
be done by following the required entries. In the General Fund book,
the entry is:
Account Name Debit Credit
Transfers from GF of Unspent DRRMF to
the TF xx
Cash in bank - LCCA xx
#
While in the Trust Fund book, the entry
would be:
Cash in bank - LCCA xx
Trust Liability-DRRMF xx
• Ensure that DRRM Utilization Report contains all sources of funding
(e.g. supplemental budgets) to present the total appropriation and
utilization of the fund.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and
Development Office ensure the inclusion of all the on-going as well as all
projects that are to be implemented and are yet to be implemented for the
reporting year to warrant transparency and provide auditable information
and verification of other stakeholders of the government.

In addition, establish / conduct monitoring within its office on the
implementation of all PPAs of the Municipality and thereafter prepare the
monitoring/utilization report and ensure that the utilization amount in the
report coincides with the records of the accounting office.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following:
For the MPDC:
a. Revisit the LDIP 2019-2022 and include provisions of PPAs for
LDRRMF based on the needs-assessment of the Municipality in
accordance to the long-term objectives of the LGU
For the CDRRMO, in coordination with the Local Disaster Risk Deduction
Management Council (LDRRMC), to:
a. Ensure that only the PPAs culled from the Local Development
Investment Plan are included in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure
that the implemented PPAs contributed to the achievement of long-
term goals in managing hazard vulnerabilities and risks.
b. Conduct thorough situational analysis (research and development) of
the Municipality to understand risk information which includes
national hazards, vulnerabilities and climate change risks for
development to alleviate poverty and environmental protection
consistent with the purpose of the fund to identify immediately needed
activities to prioritize PPAs incorporated in the LDIP; and
c. Harmonize local planning, investment programming, revenue
administration, budgeting, and expenditure management guidelines
prescribed in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

Therefore, we recommended and the LDRRMO, in coordination with the
LDRRMC, agreed to:
a. Allocate enough funds from the General Fund (GF) to subsidize the
maintenance and other operating expenses (MOOE) of the CDRRM
Office, in order not to lessen the fund intended for disaster related
activities.
b. Revisit the MDRRM Plan and make the necessary adjustments to
ensure alignment of PPAs with JMC No. 2014-1 of the NDRRMC,
DILG, DBM and CSC especially on the administrative expenses and
unrelated PPAs which do not partake the very essence of the
establishment of the Fund.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following:
For the Municipal Engineer to:
• Present actual accomplishment percentage of any project being
implemented in the Accomplishment Report
• Recommend for the imposition of liquidated damages on the upcoming
payments to the contractor (Hanna Via Construction, Inc.,) of the
project or resort to forfeiture of the contractor’s performance security
• Ensure completion of project per specification and its purpose before
recommending to the Local Chief Executive the acceptance of the
project
• Monitor strict implementation of the project as scheduled
For the Municipal Mayor to:
• Implement sanctions to the contractor (Hanna Via Construction, Inc.)
pursuant to the rules and regulations set in items 8.1 and 8.4, Annex E
of the IRR of RA 9184

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following:
For the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in
coordination with the Municipal Development Council, to:
• Identify and indicate the specificity of the prioritized projects in the
Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for the guidance of the implementing
and monitoring office in ensuring that no delays will be incurred for
the implementation of the prioritized projects. This should be coupled
with the assurance that program of works and documentary
requirements are already approved prior to the inclusion of the
projects in the AIP.
• Consider all budgetary requirements (e.g. loan amortization) before
finalizing the AIP for the budgetary year
• Announce early the request for proposal of Barangays on PPAs for
developmental projects to be implemented in their locality. Again,
POW should be readily available for evaluation.
• Conduct needs-assessment of the Municipality in terms of socio-
economic development and environmental management and include
related PPAs in the AIP
• Align AIP with PPAs culled from the Local Development Investment
Plan
For the Municipal Engineer to:
• Prepare the program of work of PPAs prior to inclusion of PPAs to the
AIP for 20% Development Fund
• Expedite implementation of PPAs as planned
For the Municipal Mayor:
• Augment manpower needs of the Municipal Engineering Office as
well as Municipal Planning and Development Office

Therefore, we recommended that the LDRRMO to do the following:
• Refocus PPAs under the MDRRMF and ensure that disaster related
PPAs are included in the AIP
• Fast tract the implementation of the PPAs ensuring compliance of the
same to the allowable PPAs under the Fund
• If PPAs are not viable for implementation, recommend realigning
appropriation to a more effective and efficient disaster related
mitigation PPAs
• Closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects funded out of
LDRRMF to avoid/minimize disaster projected and unexpected risks
and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all
levels.
• Utilize the unexpended portion of the Trust Fund of DRRMF and
prepare a comprehensive MDRRMF Plan to include both the current
appropriations and unexpended balance in the Special Trust Fund.
Management’s comment:
The management took note of the audit observation and shall take the necessary
steps to abide by the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Facilitate the preparation of BRS for all depository accounts maintained by
the Municipality.
b. Present in details all the reconciling items of every bank accounts maintained.
c. Enroll the local government unit in electronic or online banking (We-access
for LBP and EC Banking for DBP) in order to obtain information online
regarding cash balances on a given date.
2. For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Facilitate the preparation of cashbooks for all depository accounts maintained
by the Municipality.
b. Require the cashbook to be updated and balanced daily to keep track of the
changes and balances of cash per day.
3. For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Reconcile records every month to mitigate or lessen reconciling items.
b. Locate and investigate differences and prepare JEVs, if warranted.

We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
1. For the GSO to:
a. Develop procedures to mitigate and reduce reconciling balances of PPE in order
to report an accurate and complete balance of the account;
b. Complete the conduct of physical count to enable reconciliation thereof; and,
c. Locate property records and information pertaining local roads prior to CY 2015
to aid the Accounting Office.
2. For the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Make written representation to inform the system provider (IOTech) regarding the
non-recognition of depreciation expense of some PPE accounts;
b. Investigate and reconcile the difference between the balance per subsidiary ledger
and balance per physical count and make adjusting entries to correct the balances
thereof; and,
c. Exert extra effort in locating data from the RPI to enable the Municipality to have
an initial information of local roads needed to be recorded.
3. For the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Initiate the preparation of Road Map in coordination with the GSO; and,
b. Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road projects
4. Require the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to conduct
periodic reconciliation on their respective records in order to have a fair presentation
of the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements.
5. For the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the Department Heads to comply the
requirements relative to the Property, Plant and Equipment circulars / regulations.

We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Identify bottlenecks or problems in collection efforts and consider thereof
during revenue estimates.
b. Enhance forecasting of revenue collection in order to adopt a more realistic
and feasible budget.
c. Exert extra effort in revenue collection to enable the Municipality to reach its
targeted income.
2. For the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Adopt a more conservative budget to ensure that the projects, programs, and
activities incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan is appropriately funded.
b. Explore other sources of revenues such as operation of other feasible local
economic enterprise to augment the current level of income and improve the
fiscal performance of the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed on the following:
1. For the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
a. Require the Barangay Governments to submit their proposals with complete
details before the completion of Annual Investment Plan.
b. Initiate the preparation of necessary documents (eg. detailed engineering) to
support cost estimate of projects funded under the 20% Development Plan before
the budget preparation.
c. Develop and recommend other procedures to enhance the current practice
regarding the planning and budgeting of the 20% Development Plan to avoid
generic and lump-sum projects.
2. For the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Stop the practice of implementing generic or lump-sum projects without further
authority from the Sanggunian to avoid future suspension/disallowance.
b. Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the
identified priority projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund
including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target
to avoid generic and lump-sum projects.

observations.recommendation: Require the collectors to liquidate their collections daily, regardless of amount. Likewise, direct the Liquidating Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to be prudent in their duties and responsibilities and closely monitor the remittances/liquidations of the collectors. Also, comply strictly with the provisions of Section 26 and 31 of COA Circular No.92-382 dated July 3, 1992, to prevent misuse or malversation of government funds or loss of cash through theft or robbery.
observations.reason: Collections were not remitted daily.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Service Officer to explain/justify in writing why the deficiencies existed. Correct/Submit the required documents in order to comply with the requirements set forth under R.A. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Organize the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) in accordance with the staffing pattern provided under RA 10121 and the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM & CSC.

Submit a Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, prepared and certified correct by the Local Accountant to the COA Auditor in the required format on or before the 15th day of each month, through the LDRRMC and Local development Councils (LDCs).
observations.reason: No monthly report was received by the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of the reports and accounts for purposes of timely post-audit and review of financial operations as required under Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and related COA issuances.
observations.reason: Some reports are still delayed.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2006 prescribing the rules and regulations in the settlement of accounts. 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned employees, requiring them to settle their accounts immediately.
observations.reason: Verification necessary to implement this is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned employees requiring them to settle their accounts immediately.
observations.reason: Pending due to incomplete documents.

observations.recommendation: Submit properly the reports and accounts for all funds pursuant to Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 7.3 of COA Circular No. 94-006 dated February 17, 1994.
observations.reason: Some reports are still delayed.

observations.recommendation: Strengthen the accounting controls over receivables by requiring concerned personnel to liquidate cash advances after the travel had been undertaken or at the end of time-bound activities and see to it that all cash advances granted are fully settled at the end of the year. Otherwise, withhold any money due them to settle their obligations as authorized under Section 37.
observations.reason: Some cash advances were not yet liquidated.
